name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.06.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.06.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 29.06.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 29.06.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 25 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 182.72 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 236.46 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 25 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 182.72 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 236.46 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Chhattisgarh, Odisha, East & West UP, Haryana-Chandigarh-Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, East MP, Saurashtra & Kutch and Vidarbha.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Chhattisgarh, Odisha, East & West UP, Haryana-Chandigarh-Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, East MP, Saurashtra & Kutch and Vidarbha.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  June  01-  24  June  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  42% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: (-) 41%; (ii) Northwest India: -22%; (iii) Central India: -59%; and South Peninsula: -28%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  June  01-  24  June  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  42% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: (-) 41%; (ii) Northwest India: -22%; (iii) Central India: -59%; and South Peninsula: -28%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Very heavy to extremely Heavy rainfall likely over northeast India and over Sub-Himalayan till 30th June and Goa, Konkan, Madhya Maharashtra & Vidarbha, Karnataka and MP till 5 July 2026.
texts.text:  Very heavy to extremely Heavy rainfall likely over northeast India and over Sub-Himalayan till 30th June and Goa, Konkan, Madhya Maharashtra & Vidarbha, Karnataka and MP till 5 July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Moderate to severe thunderstorm activity accompanied by gusty winds, with moderate to severe Lightning activity is likely over North India, Jharkhand, Odisha, Marathwada and some parts of North-west India.
texts.text:  Moderate to severe thunderstorm activity accompanied by gusty winds, with moderate to severe Lightning activity is likely over North India, Jharkhand, Odisha, Marathwada and some parts of North-west India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave conditions likely over Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Chandigarh and Delhi till 30 th June 2026.
texts.text:  Heat wave conditions likely over Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Chandigarh and Delhi till 30 th June 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from Punjab to Bihar now runs Punjab to Gangetic West Bengal across Haryana, Uttar Pradesh and Bihar.
texts.text:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from Punjab to Bihar now runs Punjab to Gangetic West Bengal across Haryana, Uttar Pradesh and Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country. A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 2 nd July 2026.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country. A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 2 nd July 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  25  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 48.405 billion cubic meter, which is 26.37% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 73.21 percent of last  year's  storage  and  105.67  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  25  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 48.405 billion cubic meter, which is 26.37% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 73.21 percent of last  year's  storage  and  105.67  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels: In January, 2026, around 75.8% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are  observed  in  around  13.5%  of  stations  of  the  country  covering  mainly  the  western  states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels: In January, 2026, around 75.8% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are  observed  in  around  13.5%  of  stations  of  the  country  covering  mainly  the  western  states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.
texts.text: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize  -vegetative  stage  (Downy  mildew (Peronosclerospora  sorghi) )  in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize  -vegetative  stage  (Downy  mildew (Peronosclerospora  sorghi) )  in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (18 June - 24 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 47%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 24 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 42%.
texts.text:  During the week (18 June - 24 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 47%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 24 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 42%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, the conditions are likely to become favorable for further advance of the Southwest Monsoon during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, the conditions are likely to become favorable for further advance of the Southwest Monsoon during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over the entire country during the week
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over the entire country during the week

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (25 June - 01 July 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (25 June - 01 July 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures  likely  to  rise  by  2-3°C  over  Northwest  India  till  28th  and  fall  by  3-5°C thereafter.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures  likely  to  rise  by  2-3°C  over  Northwest  India  till  28th  and  fall  by  3-5°C thereafter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures likely to rise by 2-4°C over Central India till 28th and no significant change thereafter.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures likely to rise by 2-4°C over Central India till 28th and no significant change thereafter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 01st July, 2026.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 01st July, 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (02 July - 08 July 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (02 July - 08 July 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant Heatwave conditions are likely over any part of the country during the week.
texts.text:  No significant Heatwave conditions are likely over any part of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over many parts of the plains of northwest India, and some parts of East India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during week 2.
texts.text:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over many parts of the plains of northwest India, and some parts of East India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during week 2.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No  Warm  Night  conditions  are  likely  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the  week.  However, minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) over parts of northwest India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, & Uttar Pradesh), and near Normal (-1.5 to +1.5°C) across the rest of the country.
texts.text:  No  Warm  Night  conditions  are  likely  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the  week.  However, minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) over parts of northwest India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, & Uttar Pradesh), and near Normal (-1.5 to +1.5°C) across the rest of the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 20°N/60°E, 20°N/65°E, 20°N/70°E, Surat, Indore, Mandla, Daltonganj, Motihari and 28.3°N/83°E as on 28th June.
texts.text:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 20°N/60°E, 20°N/65°E, 20°N/70°E, Surat, Indore, Mandla, Daltonganj, Motihari and 28.3°N/83°E as on 28th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of North Arabian Sea, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, remaining parts of Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand & Bihar, some parts  of  Uttar  Pradesh  &  Uttarakhand  during  next  2-3  days  and  over  some  more  parts  of  Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh and southeast Rajasthan during subsequent 2-3 days.
texts.text:  Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of North Arabian Sea, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, remaining parts of Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand & Bihar, some parts  of  Uttar  Pradesh  &  Uttarakhand  during  next  2-3  days  and  over  some  more  parts  of  Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh and southeast Rajasthan during subsequent 2-3 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. CROPS
texts.text: 2. CROPS

texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Sugarcane :  About 57.31 lakh ha area coverage (106% of Normal area) has been reported compared to 56.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Sugarcane :  About 57.31 lakh ha area coverage (106% of Normal area) has been reported compared to 56.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cotton :  About  29.66  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  45.36  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Cotton :  About  29.66  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  45.36  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Jute :  About  6.25  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.13  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Jute :  About  6.25  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.13  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: * Other pulses (Other pulses- Kulthi & Mothbean), ** Average of 2022-23 & 2023-24)
texts.text: * Other pulses (Other pulses- Kulthi & Mothbean), ** Average of 2022-23 & 2023-24)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 25 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 48.405 BCM (26.37%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 25 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 48.405 BCM (26.37%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  111 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.
texts.text:  111 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  26 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.
texts.text:  26 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  29 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.
texts.text:  29 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.

texts.text: Source: CWC

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION
texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 20 June - 26 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major parts of Rajasthan, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and J&K.
texts.text:  Soil moisture during 20 June - 26 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major parts of Rajasthan, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and J&K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh.
texts.text:  Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Maharashtra,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha, Madhya  Pradesh,  Northern  Karnataka,  Telangana,  Jharkhand,  Bihar,  Gujarat,  Eastern  & Bundelkhand part of Uttar Pradesh, and in the Northeastern parts of country.
texts.text:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Maharashtra,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha, Madhya  Pradesh,  Northern  Karnataka,  Telangana,  Jharkhand,  Bihar,  Gujarat,  Eastern  & Bundelkhand part of Uttar Pradesh, and in the Northeastern parts of country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:
texts.text: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 25.06.2026 is given below:
texts.text: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 25.06.2026 is given below:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (Unit : in Lakh MT)
texts.text: (Unit : in Lakh MT)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT
texts.text: In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 25 th June, 2026 the availability of DAP is  32.25 LMT against the requirement of 10.92 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.
texts.text: As on 25 th June, 2026 the availability of DAP is  32.25 LMT against the requirement of 10.92 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 25 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (131.08 LMT), DAP (32.25 LMT), MOP (12.80 LMT), NPK (74.68 LMT) and SSP (35.56 LMT) is available.
texts.text: As on 25 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (131.08 LMT), DAP (32.25 LMT), MOP (12.80 LMT), NPK (74.68 LMT) and SSP (35.56 LMT) is available.
